_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Knowledge of personnel systems and software
reference_document_element_description: Knowledge of metadata types (business, technical, operational, process) and the systems and methods used to collect, store, and maintain each type.
comments: NICE knowledge intersects with WIDAI DQ domain. Knowledge DQ-K-010 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: TF-S-011 (TF, cosine: 0.521), LS-K-009 (LS, cosine: 0.495).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.2/10 (raw 0.424). Shared vocabulary: [knowledge, systems]. NICE-only terms [personnel, software] vs WIDAI-only terms [business, collect, maintain, metadata, methods, operational] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.892 (P=0.924, R=0.861). P exceeds R by 0.063 — the NICE element's 6 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 21 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (93.2%). "Knowledge of personnel systems and software" and "Knowledge of metadata types (business, technical, operational, process) and the" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.559 > STS 0.424 (gap +0.135). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [knowledge, systems] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 19.1%. Shared tokens: [knowledge, systems]. NICE-only: [personnel, software]. WIDAI-only: [business, collect, maintain, metadata, methods, operational].

signals.detail: STS 0.424 barely clears classification threshold for Superset of. This match is fragile and may indicate insufficient KSA coverage for this competency area.
Bi-encoder/STS gap 0.135 suggests some vocabulary-driven inflation in candidate selection.
